scope: To understand the applications of Biostatistical and Research Methodology in Pharmaceutics, Pharmacokinetics, and Clinical trials.
objectives: Understand the application of statistical methods in pharmacy
Explain the design of research studies and clinical trials
Apply statistical calculations and tests to drug data
courseOutcomes: CO1: Apply descriptive and graphical statistics to clinical and analytical drug data.
CO2: Perform hypothesis testing using t-test, ANOVA, and Chi-square test.
CO3: Formulate experimental designs like Factorial designs and Response Surface methodology.
CO4: Comprehend observational and experimental research methodology designs.
CO5: Discuss data analysis software usage in pharmaceutical research.

topics.topicName: Introduction: Definition, application, role of statistics in pharmacy, collection, classification, and tabulation of data.
topics.name: Introduction: Definition, application, role of statistics in pharmacy, collection, classification, and tabulation of data.

topics.topicName: Measures of central tendency: Mean, median, mode. Measures of dispersion: Standard deviation, standard error, variance, coefficient of variation.
topics.name: Measures of central tendency: Mean, median, mode. Measures of dispersion: Standard deviation, standard error, variance, coefficient of variation.

topics.topicName: Parametric tests: Student t-test (paired and unpaired), F-test, ANOVA (one-way and two-way), and multiple comparison tests.
topics.name: Parametric tests: Student t-test (paired and unpaired), F-test, ANOVA (one-way and two-way), and multiple comparison tests.

topics.topicName: Non-parametric tests: Chi-square test, Wilcoxon signed rank test, Mann-Whitney U test.
topics.name: Non-parametric tests: Chi-square test, Wilcoxon signed rank test, Mann-Whitney U test.

topics.topicName: Experimental Design: Factorial designs (2^2 and 2^3), Plackett-Burman design, central composite design.
topics.name: Experimental Design: Factorial designs (2^2 and 2^3), Plackett-Burman design, central composite design.

topics.topicName: Response Surface Methodology: Contour plots, 3D response curves, and pharmaceutical formulation optimization.
topics.name: Response Surface Methodology: Contour plots, 3D response curves, and pharmaceutical formulation optimization.

topics.topicName: Research Design: Observation, cohort, randomized controlled clinical trials (RCTs), historical control, design aspects.
topics.name: Research Design: Observation, cohort, randomized controlled clinical trials (RCTs), historical control, design aspects.

topics.topicName: Ethics & Reporting: Informed consent, Helsinki declaration, reporting standards of clinical trials.
topics.name: Ethics & Reporting: Informed consent, Helsinki declaration, reporting standards of clinical trials.

topics.topicName: Correlation and Regression: Linear correlation, Pearson correlation coefficient, simple and multiple linear regression.
topics.name: Correlation and Regression: Linear correlation, Pearson correlation coefficient, simple and multiple linear regression.

topics.topicName: Statistical Softwares: Brief introduction to SPSS, Minitab, and Excel statistical functions.
topics.name: Statistical Softwares: Brief introduction to SPSS, Minitab, and Excel statistical functions.

recommendedBooks.author: Sanford Bolton & Charles Bon
recommendedBooks.title: Pharmaceutical Statistics: Practical and Clinical Applications

referenceBooks.author: Wayne W. Daniel
referenceBooks.title: Biostatistics: A Foundation for Analysis in the Health Sciences

scope: The purpose of this course is to introduce to students a number of health issues and their challenges, and explain the roles of preventive pharmacy in public health.
objectives: Acquire high-level consciousness of global health problems
Understand preventive measures for infectious and non-communicable diseases
Describe national health programs and policies in India
courseOutcomes: CO1: Comprehend concepts of health, disease prevention, and nutritional hygiene.
CO2: Describe etiology, transmission, and prevention of cholera, tuberculosis, malaria, and HIV/AIDS.
CO3: Formulate preventive and control measures for non-communicable diseases like cancer, diabetes, and hypertension.
CO4: Explain National Health Programs (pulse polio, tobacco control, malaria control) and their impacts.
CO5: Discuss community health services and primary health center (PHC) organizations.

topics.topicName: Health Concepts: Definition of health, dimensions, determinants, indicators of health, disease transmission.
topics.name: Health Concepts: Definition of health, dimensions, determinants, indicators of health, disease transmission.

topics.topicName: Nutrition & Hygiene: Nutritional deficiencies, balanced diet, food hygiene, personal hygiene, and environmental sanitation.
topics.name: Nutrition & Hygiene: Nutritional deficiencies, balanced diet, food hygiene, personal hygiene, and environmental sanitation.

topics.topicName: Infectious Diseases: General principles of prevention and control of cholera, typhoid, tuberculosis, malaria, dengue, and influenza.
topics.name: Infectious Diseases: General principles of prevention and control of cholera, typhoid, tuberculosis, malaria, dengue, and influenza.

topics.topicName: Sexually Transmitted Infections: Hepatitis, HIV/AIDS, epidemiology, control measures.
topics.name: Sexually Transmitted Infections: Hepatitis, HIV/AIDS, epidemiology, control measures.

topics.topicName: Non-communicable Diseases: Prevention, screening, and management of cancer, diabetes mellitus, cardiovascular diseases, and hypertension.
topics.name: Non-communicable Diseases: Prevention, screening, and management of cancer, diabetes mellitus, cardiovascular diseases, and hypertension.

topics.topicName: Risk Factors: Substance abuse, tobacco, alcohol, drug addictions, obesity, and mental health.
topics.name: Risk Factors: Substance abuse, tobacco, alcohol, drug addictions, obesity, and mental health.

topics.topicName: National Programs: Pulse Polio, National Tuberculosis Elimination Program (NTEP), National Malaria Control Program.
topics.name: National Programs: Pulse Polio, National Tuberculosis Elimination Program (NTEP), National Malaria Control Program.

topics.topicName: Welfare Programs: Family planning, maternal and child health, reproductive and child health (RCH) programs, tobacco control program.
topics.name: Welfare Programs: Family planning, maternal and child health, reproductive and child health (RCH) programs, tobacco control program.

topics.topicName: PHC & Community: Functions and organization of Primary Health Center (PHC), Community Health Center (CHC), rural health services.
topics.name: PHC & Community: Functions and organization of Primary Health Center (PHC), Community Health Center (CHC), rural health services.

topics.topicName: NGOs: Role of National and International NGOs, WHO, UNICEF, Red Cross in public health.
topics.name: NGOs: Role of National and International NGOs, WHO, UNICEF, Red Cross in public health.

recommendedBooks.author: K. Park
recommendedBooks.title: Park's Textbook of Preventive and Social Medicine

referenceBooks.author: Prabhakara G.N.
referenceBooks.title: A Textbook of Preventive and Social Medicine

scope: The course is designed to impart marketing concepts and strategies applicable to pharmaceutical industries.
objectives: Understand core concepts of pharmaceutical marketing management
Formulate product design, pricing, and distribution channel structures
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain pharmaceutical marketing concepts, environments, and consumer behaviors.
CO2: Understand product branding, packaging, and product life cycle (PLC) management.
CO3: Describe pharmaceutical pricing methods and selection of distribution channels.

topics.topicName: Pharma Marketing: Definition, scope, traditional vs modern marketing concepts, macro/micro environments.
topics.name: Pharma Marketing: Definition, scope, traditional vs modern marketing concepts, macro/micro environments.

topics.topicName: Consumer behavior: Models of buying behavior, factors influencing prescribing habits of physicians.
topics.name: Consumer behavior: Models of buying behavior, factors influencing prescribing habits of physicians.

topics.topicName: Product management: Branding, packaging, labeling, product life cycle, new product development.
topics.name: Product management: Branding, packaging, labeling, product life cycle, new product development.

scope: This course is designed to impart a fundamental knowledge on the regulatory requirements for drug approval.
objectives: Understand regulatory affairs, guidelines, and international drug submissions
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain drug development and clinical phase regulations.
CO2: Describe drug submission protocols and CTD dossier formatting.

topics.topicName: Regulatory requirements: Pre-clinical studies, clinical trials, NDA, IND, ANDA filings.
topics.name: Regulatory requirements: Pre-clinical studies, clinical trials, NDA, IND, ANDA filings.

scope: The subject is designed to provide an overview of safety monitoring of drugs.
objectives: Describe the principles of pharmacovigilance and drug safety surveillance
courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand ADR definitions, reporting tools, and database monitoring.

topics.topicName: ADR Reporting: Reporting procedures, dictionaries, codes, databases, WHO center operations.
topics.name: ADR Reporting: Reporting procedures, dictionaries, codes, databases, WHO center operations.

courseName: Quality Control and Standardization of Herbals - Theory
scope: To impart guidelines for quality control and standardization of raw materials and herbal drug products.
objectives: Describe herbal drug quality validation and pharmacognostic standards
courseOutcomes: CO1: Perform quality evaluation of herbal formulations according to WHO guidelines.

topics.topicName: Quality Control: WHO guidelines on quality control of herbal drugs, ash value, moisture, extractive values.
topics.name: Quality Control: WHO guidelines on quality control of herbal drugs, ash value, moisture, extractive values.

scope: This course is designed to impart fundamental knowledge on drug design and discovery.
objectives: Explain the techniques of computer aided drug design (CADD) and docking
courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand QSAR modeling, docking protocols, and virtual screening.

topics.topicName: QSAR: 2D QSAR, 3D QSAR, molecular modeling, protein-ligand docking methodologies.
topics.name: QSAR: 2D QSAR, 3D QSAR, molecular modeling, protein-ligand docking methodologies.

scope: Cell biology provides a basic understanding of the chemical, structural and functional properties of cells.
objectives: Explain cellular structures, DNA replication, and gene expression
courseOutcomes: CO1: Comprehend cellular signals, genomic transcriptions, and translational biochemistry.

topics.topicName: Cell Structure: Organelles, DNA replication, transcription, and translation, cell cycle regulation.
topics.name: Cell Structure: Organelles, DNA replication, transcription, and translation, cell cycle regulation.

scope: This course is designed to impart fundamental knowledge on formulation, manufacturing, and evaluation of cosmetics.
objectives: Describe skin anatomy in relation to cosmetics and formulation of creams, shampoos, and lotions
courseOutcomes: CO1: Formulate cosmetics and evaluate stability of standard preparations.

topics.topicName: Cosmetics: Classifications, formulation of skin care (creams, face powders) and hair care products (shampoos).
topics.name: Cosmetics: Classifications, formulation of skin care (creams, face powders) and hair care products (shampoos).

scope: This course is designed to impart fundamental knowledge on experimental animal screening techniques.
objectives: Explain the animal ethical guidelines and common biological screening methods
courseOutcomes: CO1: Describe pre-clinical animal screening methods and CPCSEA guidelines.

topics.topicName: CPCSEA: Guidelines for breeding and usage of animals, screening for CNS and CVS active drugs.
topics.name: CPCSEA: Guidelines for breeding and usage of animals, screening for CNS and CVS active drugs.

scope: This course is designed to impart advanced knowledge on NMR, Mass Spectrometry, and Thermal analytical methods.
objectives: Understand the theory, instrumentation and applications of NMR and Mass spectrometry
courseOutcomes: CO1: Interpret NMR and Mass spectra and understand DSC/TGA thermal analyses.

topics.topicName: Advanced Spectrophotometry: NMR theory and Mass fragmentation, applications, thermal analysis (DSC, TGA).
topics.name: Advanced Spectrophotometry: NMR theory and Mass fragmentation, applications, thermal analysis (DSC, TGA).

scope: This subject covers the nutritional and therapeutic values of dietary supplements.
objectives: Identify the health benefits of phytochemicals and dietary supplements
courseOutcomes: CO1: Categorize nutraceuticals and explain their roles in disease preventions.

topics.topicName: Nutraceuticals: Definition, types, health benefits in diabetes, cancer, and age-related disorders.
topics.name: Nutraceuticals: Definition, types, health benefits in diabetes, cancer, and age-related disorders.

scope: This course is designed to provide details of preformulation and formulation optimization.
objectives: Understand preformulation factors and formulation parameters for pharmaceuticals
courseOutcomes: CO1: Describe preformulation parameters, excipient selections, and optimization studies.

topics.topicName: Preformulation studies: Physical and chemical properties, excipients compatibility and optimization methods.
topics.name: Preformulation studies: Physical and chemical properties, excipients compatibility and optimization methods.
